mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate above 5% signals weakening labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal tightening monetary policy and rising risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the combined effect of CPI and FEDFUNDS on SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of supply-side disruption, declining industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates stagflationary pressures that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO declines and CPI rises.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 60%+ of instances when INDPRO rises.

mechanism_narrative: Strong job growth (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 60%+ of instances when PAYEMS rises.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts (HOUST) signal strong demand for residential construction, which boosts economic growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 60%+ of instances when HOUST rises.

mechanism_narrative: Combined growth in real sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer and business demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the combined effect of RSAFS and HOUST on SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).
